questions.q: In which year was the Battle of Talikota fought?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Talikota was fought in 1565 CE, ending Vijayanagara's golden age.

questions.q: Which empire was defeated at the Battle of Talikota?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Talikota ended in a crushing defeat for the Vijayanagara Empire.

questions.q: The forces opposing Vijayanagara at Talikota were an alliance of which kind of states?
questions.explanation: An alliance of Deccan sultanates combined to defeat Vijayanagara at Talikota.

questions.q: Which famous Vijayanagara capital was sacked after the Battle of Talikota?
questions.explanation: The victorious armies sacked the great capital of Hampi after the battle.

questions.q: The Battle of Talikota is best described as which kind of historical event?
questions.explanation: Talikota was a decisive land battle between Vijayanagara and the Deccan sultanate alliance.

questions.q: In which part of the Indian subcontinent did the Battle of Talikota take place?
options: The Deccan region of southern India
questions.explanation: Talikota lies in the Deccan, where Vijayanagara and the sultanates contended for power.

questions.q: What broad outcome did the Battle of Talikota have for Vijayanagara?
options: It united it with the sultanates
It led to its irreversible decline
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara never fully recovered from the defeat and the sack of its capital.

questions.q: The Deccan sultanates that fought at Talikota were formerly part of which earlier kingdom?
questions.explanation: The five Deccan sultanates emerged from the fragmentation of the earlier Bahmani Sultanate.

questions.q: On which river's vicinity, near the Krishna, was the Battle of Talikota fought?
questions.explanation: The battle was fought near the Krishna river in the northern Deccan.

questions.q: The Battle of Talikota is also commonly known by what other name?
questions.explanation: The engagement is also called the Battle of Rakshasi-Tangadi, after nearby villages.

questions.q: Who was the aged regent of Vijayanagara who commanded its army at Talikota?
questions.explanation: Rama Raya, the powerful regent of Vijayanagara, led its forces and was killed in the battle.

questions.q: How many Deccan sultanates formed the main alliance against Vijayanagara at Talikota?
questions.explanation: Four of the Deccan sultanates, including Bijapur, Ahmadnagar, Golconda and Bidar, allied against Vijayanagara.

questions.q: Which dynasty did the reigning emperor Sadashiva Raya belong to at the time of the Battle of Talikota?
questions.explanation: Sadashiva Raya, the emperor at Talikota in 1565, belonged to the Tuluva dynasty; the Aravidu dynasty was founded only after his death in 1570, though Rama Raya wielded real power.

questions.q: Which Deccan sultanate was a key member of the alliance and was based at Bijapur?
questions.explanation: The Adil Shahi sultanate of Bijapur was a principal member of the anti-Vijayanagara alliance.

questions.q: What reportedly contributed decisively to the collapse of the Vijayanagara army at Talikota?
options: The defection of Muslim commanders in its ranks
questions.explanation: The reported defection of Muslim commanders in Vijayanagara's service helped turn the battle.

questions.q: After Talikota, to which new centre did the surviving Vijayanagara rulers shift their base?
questions.explanation: The Aravidu rulers relocated their capital to Penukonda after Hampi was sacked.

questions.q: Which sultanate of the alliance was the diamond-trading state seated at Golconda?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Shahi sultanate of Golconda was part of the alliance that defeated Vijayanagara.

questions.q: The famed ruins of Hampi, ravaged after Talikota, today carry what international designation?
questions.explanation: The ruins of Hampi, the sacked capital, are recognised as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: Talikota came only a few decades after the reign of which celebrated Vijayanagara emperor?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya, who ruled until 1529, had led Vijayanagara to its peak shortly before Talikota.

questions.q: Following its defeat, how long did the sacking and plundering of Hampi reportedly continue?
questions.explanation: Contemporary accounts describe the looting of Hampi continuing for months after the battle.

questions.q: Which present-day Indian state contains the site of Talikota?
questions.explanation: Talikota lies in present-day Karnataka, in the northern Deccan.

questions.q: What role had Rama Raya played in Deccan politics before Talikota that helped provoke the alliance?
options: He had stayed neutral in all disputes
He had interfered in the sultanates' rivalries to Vijayanagara's advantage
He had converted to a new faith
questions.explanation: Rama Raya's repeated meddling in the sultanates' quarrels helped unite them against Vijayanagara.

questions.q: Which Nizam Shahi ruler of Ahmadnagar was a leading figure in the alliance at Talikota?
questions.explanation: Husain Nizam Shah I of Ahmadnagar was a prominent leader of the allied Deccan forces.

questions.q: Which single Deccan sultanate is generally said to have stayed apart from the main four-power alliance at Talikota?
questions.explanation: Berar (the Imad Shahi sultanate) is usually cited as the one Deccan state not part of the main alliance.

questions.q: Roughly how old was Rama Raya, the Vijayanagara commander, said to be at the time of Talikota?
questions.explanation: Rama Raya was an aged leader, reported in many accounts to be around seventy at Talikota.

questions.q: Which Portuguese-influenced chronicles are an important source on Vijayanagara around the era of Talikota?
options: The accounts of Nuniz and Paes
questions.explanation: The Portuguese travellers Fernao Nuniz and Domingo Paes left valuable accounts of late Vijayanagara.

questions.q: After Rama Raya was captured at Talikota, what fate befell him?
options: He was executed on the battlefield
questions.explanation: Rama Raya was captured and executed during the battle, breaking Vijayanagara's resistance.

questions.q: The Qutb Shahi ruler of Golconda who joined the alliance was named what?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Qutb Shah of Golconda was among the sultans who allied against Vijayanagara at Talikota.

questions.q: What technological advantage is often credited to the Deccan sultanates' forces at Talikota?
questions.explanation: The allied sultanates are often credited with effective use of artillery and gunpowder weapons.

questions.q: Which brother of Rama Raya managed to lead survivors away and preserve a remnant Vijayanagara state?
questions.explanation: Tirumala Deva Raya, Rama Raya's brother, withdrew with the puppet king Sadashiva and re-established rule at Penukonda.
